seoTitle: Rawson vs Raywood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rawson and Raywood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rawson and Raywood.
heading: Rawson vs Raywood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rawson, Victoria and Raywood, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rawson and Raywood have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Rawson or Raywood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rawson has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Raywood scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rawson scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Raywood scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rawson or Raywood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rawson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
